The question of securing separa-
ton OF the Northern Pacific tracks
from grade level in this city is now
up to the opponents of the plan pro-
posed by the railroad company.


Without asking further consid-
Grafton Attorney Cannon last night
withdrew the company's applica-
ton for a franchise and discon-
tinued any former efforts. On mo-
ton of Councilman Lambert the ap-
plication was placed on file and
that ends the matter as far as the
railroad or the council are con-
cerned for the present. The rail-
road simply withdraws its plans
AMI proposals and leaves the city to
do as 1t likes.


Property owners along the right
of way and in Brownies addition
have blocked it with a protest, and
the question Is left no nearer solu-
ton than before. Cannon stated
that he acted upon direct and con-
orders from headquarters.
The council has no disposition to
take up the matter again in the
immediate future at least, and the
is general in the cham
Der that any attempt to secure the
desired separation will prove
futile.


Attorney Cannon states that
there is no chance of the matter
being reopened by the Northern
Pacific so far as he knows.


The property owners interested
in fighting the dirt fill are not GER
tain what can be done in regard
to securing a better plan of separa-
ton, but they are figuring on the
Of the new council being
able to accomplish something along
that line.


Mayor Moore says the commits
sion he appointed on the tunnel
